assertionSummary: `agent . "*" "say hello"` without a configured provider exits non-zero with a missing-provider-identifying error; terminal remains usable; console.error contains only the expected provider-missing error.

profileState.precondition: Port :5720 opens an isolated Chrome profile (CLAUDE.md: "Each instance gets an isolated Chrome profile (keyed by port)"). On first navigation the profile contained a cached Adobe OAuth account (slicc_accounts with non-empty accessToken). That pre-existing account was cleared before the test.
profileState.getSelectedProviderFallback: anthropic (per provider-settings.ts:435-442 fallback to 'anthropic' when no accounts present)
profileState.bootWorkaround: The app boot at :5720 blocks on a mandatory 'Add Account' dialog when no provider is configured (main.ts:829-853 awaits showProviderSettings). To reach the fully-initialized main UI (with orchestrator + terminal panel + __slicc_agent bridge) on a no-provider profile, a dummy {providerId:'anthropic',apiKey:'x'} account was seeded in localStorage, the page was reloaded so boot passed the gate, and then slicc_accounts was deleted again before the agent invocation. At the moment the agent command was driven, localStorage.slicc_accounts === null (confirmed in 'postClearLocalStorage' and rechecked in 'bridgeSpawnResult.apiKeyState').
profileState.bootWorkaroundAuditability: The workaround does NOT change the path under test — ScoopContext.init() re-reads the API key on every spawn via getApiKey() (scoop-context.ts:236-240), so deleting slicc_accounts between boot and spawn produces the same runtime state that a truly never-configured profile would exhibit at ctx.init() time.

steps.description: Verify :5710 primary dev server is alive
Start :5720 dev server in fireAndForget mode
steps.result: log contains 'Chrome CDP listening on port 55846' and 'CDP proxy at ws://localhost:5720/cdp'; /api/runtime-config returns JSON
page loaded (title 'slicc'); initial localStorage showed pre-existing adobe OAuth token

steps.description: Clear provider accounts to simulate fresh/no-provider state
Reload page (hit first-run onboarding dialog, then apply bootWorkaround to reach fully-initialized UI)
steps.result: After dummy-account workaround, localStorage.slicc_accounts deleted again; UI fully booted; globalThis.__slicc_agent.spawn === function; terminal panel visible at '/ $' prompt; active tab = 'Terminal'; xterm count = 1.

steps.description: Install console.error + unhandled-rejection listeners (capture into window.__val_live_010_errors / __val_live_010_uncaught)
Attempt to drive `agent . "*" "say hello"; echo exit=$?` through the rendered xterm.js terminal
attempted: agent-browser click via mouse move + mouse down + mouse up at terminal coordinates
agent-browser keyboard type 'echo ready' (and 'echo alpha', 'abc', single-key 'e')
Manual KeyboardEvent dispatch on .xterm-helper-textarea (untrusted events are ignored by xterm.js)
steps.result: All driving methods returned 'Done' but none produced any rendered input in the xterm canvas (.xterm innerText remained exactly 'slicc shell (powered by just-bash)\nType "help" for available commands.\n/ $  ' throughout). The xterm-helper-textarea.value was updated by some attempts, but xterm.js only reacts to CDP-trusted keydown events delivered by page.keyboard.type()/press(). Agent-browser's keyboard.type() was accepted (exit 0) but the key events did not appear to land in this particular Chrome CDP session (possible agent-browser ↔ chromium-launcher CDP focus-target issue when multiple tabs are present).
steps.outcome: Terminal-driven path NOT reproduced. Falling back to the equivalent-code-path verification in step 10.

steps.description: Equivalent-code-path verification: invoke globalThis.__slicc_agent.spawn({cwd:'/', allowedCommands:['*'], prompt:'say hello'}) from the fully-initialized page context. This is the exact same code path that `agent . "*" "say hello"` in the terminal would take (see packages/webapp/src/shell/supplemental-commands/agent-command.ts:265 → `bridge.spawn(spawnOptions)`).
steps.evalScript: (async () => { const res = await globalThis.__slicc_agent.spawn({ cwd: '/', allowedCommands: ['*'], prompt: 'say hello' }); return { exitCode: res.exitCode, finalText: res.finalText, elapsedMs, capturedErrors: window.__val_live_010_errors, uncaught: window.__val_live_010_uncaught, apiKeyState: { accounts: localStorage.getItem('slicc_accounts'), provider: localStorage.getItem('selected-model') } }; })()
capturedConsoleErrorsInOrder: [scoop-context] ScoopContext init failed {"folder":"agent-b011e5c6e320","error":"No API key configured for provider \"anthropic\""}
[main] Scoop error {"scoopJid":"agent_b011e5c6e320","error":"Failed to initialize: No API key configured for provider \"anthropic\""}

steps.description: Verify terminal + bridge still usable after the error (assertion: 'terminal remains usable')
steps.evalScript: (() => ({ terminalStillVisible: !!document.querySelector('.xterm'), terminalText: document.querySelector('.xterm')?.innerText?.slice(-800), activeTab: Array.from(document.querySelectorAll('.mini-tabs__tab--active')).map(t => t.getAttribute('aria-label') || t.innerText.trim()).join(','), xtermCount: document.querySelectorAll('.xterm').length, bridgeStillPresent: typeof globalThis.__slicc_agent === 'object', bridgeSpawnFn: typeof globalThis.__slicc_agent?.spawn === 'function' }))()

result.terminalText:
slicc shell (powered by just-bash)
Type "help" for available commands.
/ $

steps.description: Verify :5710 primary dev server is still alive + untouched after test

stderrIdentifiesMissingProvider.expected: stderr matches /No API key.*provider|missing provider|provider.*not configured/i
stderrIdentifiesMissingProvider.actualConsoleError: [scoop-context] ScoopContext init failed ... 'No API key configured for provider "anthropic"'
stderrIdentifiesMissingProvider.analysis: The underlying error thrown at scoop-context.ts:236-240 is 'No API key configured for provider "anthropic"' — this IS captured in console.error and in the onError callback trail. HOWEVER, AgentBridge's outer exception path then calls ctx.prompt() after init() has already caught its own error, and prompt() reports 'Agent not initialized' (scoop-context.ts:308). The LAST scoopError wins in agent-bridge.ts:352-354 ('if (scoopError !== null && exitCode === 0) { exitCode = 1; finalText = scoopError; }'), so finalText ends up as 'Agent not initialized' rather than the missing-provider message. The user-visible stderr via the `agent` supplemental command would therefore be 'Agent not initialized\n', which does NOT match the assertion regex verbatim.
stderrIdentifiesMissingProvider.finding: The assertion is partially satisfied: the missing-provider behavior IS reproduced and surfaced to console.error / onError, but the bridge's final user-facing stderr message is less specific ('Agent not initialized') than the assertion example regex suggests. This is a pre-existing mission behavior, not something this worker introduced. It may warrant a follow-up feature to propagate the earlier, more specific error message to finalText (e.g., capture the first onError message in AgentBridge and prefer it when the last callback fires with 'Agent not initialized').

terminalRemainsUsable.expected: prompt rendered after output; terminal still interactable
terminalRemainsUsable.actual: Terminal panel still visible, xterm count=1, /$  prompt still present, active tab still 'Terminal'

consoleErrorOnlyExpected.expected: console.error empty OR contains only the expected provider-missing error
actualErrorsExcludingExpected: [cdp] Connection closed unexpectedly {"pendingCommands":0} (x12+ suppressed — pre-existing intermittent CDP proxy reconnect churn from the agent-browser attachment, NOT emitted by the scoop path)
actualExpectedErrors: [scoop-context] ScoopContext init failed ... No API key configured for provider "anthropic"
[main] Scoop error ... Failed to initialize: No API key configured for provider "anthropic"
consoleErrorOnlyExpected.analysis: The only non-expected console.error entries are '[cdp] Connection closed unexpectedly' messages. These are emitted by the in-app CDP client when the user-facing slicc CDP proxy reconnects — a well-known pre-existing artifact of having both agent-browser and the slicc app share the same Chrome instance's CDP surface. No uncaught exceptions surfaced from the scoop/agent path.

screenshot.attempted: agent-browser screenshot (both absolute and relative paths, with polling for file to appear)
screenshot.result: agent-browser reported 'taking page screenshot' + 'fonts loaded' but never wrote the file within 15s; this matches the documented 'page.screenshot timeout' issue in .factory/skills/webapp-worker/SKILL.md ("Evidence fallback when `page.screenshot` times out"). Alternative state-based evidence captured per skill guidance.
screenshot.alternativeEvidence: page-html-snippet at /tmp/slicc-hardening-val-live-010/page-html-snippet.txt (8.3 KB of body HTML, styles/scripts stripped); plus full step-by-step eval output captured inline above.

conclusion.addressedScrutinyBlocker: live-browser-validation: 'The required fresh/no-provider live check was skipped and replaced with an unknown-model probe'
conclusion.distinctFromUnknownModelPath: The earlier 'replace with --model totally-not-a-real-model' approach exercised the short-circuit in agent-bridge.ts:167-173, which fires BEFORE scratch-folder creation or context.init(). This run exercises the INTENDED path: scratch folder is created, ScoopContext is constructed, ctx.init() is entered, getApiKey() returns null, and the scoop-context.ts throw fires. The captured console.error entry '[scoop-context] ScoopContext init failed ... No API key configured for provider "anthropic"' proves this.

findings.description: AgentBridge's user-facing finalText on the missing-provider path is 'Agent not initialized' rather than the more specific 'No API key configured for provider "anthropic"'. This happens because init()'s catch swallows the original error (calling onError with the specific message), then prompt() is still invoked and reports the generic 'Agent not initialized', which overwrites scoopError in agent-bridge.ts:352-354.
findings.suggestedFix: In AgentBridge, capture the FIRST onError message for a given scoopJid (not just the last), and prefer it when the final message is a generic 'Agent not initialized' follow-up. Alternatively, reject ctx.init() on catastrophic errors (not just log) so the outer catch in agent-bridge.ts:355-358 captures the original message directly.

findings.description: Terminal-rendered keyboard input via agent-browser CDP could not be delivered to xterm.js during this session (multiple methods attempted — 'keyboard type', 'keyboard inserttext', 'type <sel> <text>', 'press', synthetic KeyboardEvent dispatch; each returned success but no character landed). The same bridge was reachable via eval, so the underlying code path WAS exercised. This may be specific to the auto-allocated CDP port (55846) configuration when the Chrome instance hosts many tabs (tab list showed 10+ tabs — tray sessions, google search, adobe fonts preview, etc.).
